s2.ptxt: Install the punch assembly to the overhaul handle, inserting it in the hole in part A as shown in the illustration.
While gripping the handle, adjust the stopper bolt so that dimension B is as shown in the illustration.
After checking that there are no foreign objects in the lock plate groove, install a new O-ring without twisting it.

atten4.ptxt: When cleaning the lock plate groove, lightly rub it with sand paper without scratching it.

s2.ptxt: Install the tank without damaging the O-ring.
Tap the lock plate with a soft-faced hammer so that there is no gap between the lock plate and the tank.

Lightly press SST against the lock plate in the order shown in the illustration. After repeating this a few times, fully caulk the lock plate by gripping the handle until stopped by the stopper plate.
Do not tap the areas indicated by dotted circles in the illustration.

atten3.ptxt: For the areas where cannot be tapped with SST, use pliers or similar objects and be careful not to damage the core plats.

s2.ptxt: Check the lock plate height (H) after completing caulking.
If not within the specified height range, adjust the stopper bolt of the handle and caulk again.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL RADIATOR TANK LOWER (for Automatic Transaxle)

s3.ptxt: Clean the O-ring contact surfaces of the lower tank and oil cooler.
Install 2 new O-rings to the oil cooler (D).
Install the oil cooler to the lower tank (C).
Install the plate washer and nut (B).
Install the cooler pipe with the bolt (A).

s2.ptxt: Installation procedure of the radiator tank lower is the same as that of the radiator tank upper.

s3.ptxt: Install the hose with the bolt and 2 clips.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL RADIATOR TANK LOWER (for Manual Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: Installation procedure of the radiator tank lower is the same as that of the radiator tank upper.
Install a new O-ring to the drain plug.
Plug the inlet and outlet pipes of the radiator with SST.
Using the radiator cap tester, apply pressure to the radiator.

atten4.ptxt: On radiators with resin tanks, there is clearance between the tank and lock plate where a minute amount of air will remain, giving the appearance of an air leak when the radiator is submerged in water. Therefore, before doing the water leak test, first swish the radiator around in the water until all air bubbles disappear.
